tasks.title: Add mode routing and exclusivity validation
tasks.acceptance: runReview detects --merge, --resolve, --resolve-all, --verify, --report boolean flags and routes to separate run functions. Mutually exclusive modes (merge+resolve, merge+verify, merge+report, resolve+verify, resolve+report, verify+report) produce exit 2 with clear error. Modifier flags (--diff-from, --spec-ref, --round, --findings, --affected-files, --final-round) rejected with merge/resolve/report modes. New flags registered in newReviewCmd(). Tests: all 6 pairwise mode exclusivity combos exit 2.
tasks.closed_reason: runReview detects --merge, --resolve, --resolve-all, --verify, --report boolean flags via detectReviewMode() and routes to runReviewMerge/runReviewResolve/runReviewResolveAll/runReviewVerify/runReviewReport stub functions. All 6 mutually exclusive mode combos (merge+resolve, merge+verify, merge+report, resolve+verify, resolve+report, verify+report) exit 2 with clear error tested in TestReviewModeMutualExclusivity. Modifier flags (--diff-from, --spec-ref, --round, --findings, --affected-files, --final-round) rejected with merge/resolve/report modes via validateModeFlags, tested in TestReviewMergeRejectsModifierFlags. All new flags registered in newReviewCmd. Default review backward compatible (TestReviewDefaultStillWorks). No spec arg exits 2 (TestReviewNoSpecArgErrors).

tasks.title: Extract finding identity key function
tasks.acceptance: findingIdentityKey(f) returns (category, location, finding_prefix) tuple where finding_prefix is first 80 chars of finding field. Function exported from a shared location (review.go or new file). Used by merge dedup and report cross-round tracking.
tasks.closed_reason: findingIdentityKey(category, location, finding) returns (category::location::finding_prefix) where finding_prefix is first 80 chars of finding field. Constant findingPrefixLen=80 defined. Function used by dedupFindings. Existing TestReviewFindingsDedup updated to test new 3-tuple dedup key: two findings with same (category, location) but same finding_prefix are deduped, different finding text are kept separate.

tasks.title: Implement merge: parse, dedup, sort NDJSON
tasks.acceptance: review_merge.go: reads NDJSON files, validates findings (must have severity+finding fields), skips blank/invalid lines with warning to stderr, deduplicates by (category, location, finding_prefix) keeping highest severity, sorts by severity then category, outputs merged NDJSON to stdout. Preserves extra fields including resolved. Single file input valid (normalize). 0 files exits 2. Missing file exits 3. All invalid exits 1. Tests: dedup with severity priority, sort order, invalid lines skipped, single file normalize, 0 files error, missing file error, all invalid error, preserves resolved field.
tasks.closed_reason: review_merge.go: runReviewMerge reads NDJSON files, validates severity+finding required, skips blank/invalid lines with warning to stderr, deduplicates by findingIdentityKey keeping highest severity, sorts by severity then category, outputs NDJSON to stdout or -o file, summary to stderr. Uses map[string]any to preserve all fields including resolved. Exit 2 for 0 files, exit 3 for missing, exit 1 for all invalid. Single file normalizes. 8 tests in review_merge_test.go covering dedup, sort, invalid lines, single normalize, zero files, missing file, preserves fields, output file, all-invalid.

tasks.title: Implement merge output: -o and --json modes
tasks.acceptance: Default: NDJSON to stdout, summary to stderr. With -o: NDJSON to file, JSON summary to stdout. With --json (no -o): JSON result with findings array to stdout. With --json and -o: NDJSON to file, JSON summary to stdout (--json redundant). TTY: always raw NDJSON to stdout (intentional exception documented). Tests: -o writes file and summary to stdout, --json includes findings array, --json with -o same as -o alone.
tasks.closed_reason: runReviewMerge now handles 3 output modes: (1) with -o: NDJSON to file, JSON summary to stdout (2) with --json no -o: JSON with findings array to stdout (3) default: raw NDJSON to stdout, summary to stderr. bySeverity breakdown computed and included in JSON summary. Uses output.JSON for structured output and IsJSONOutput for mode detection. TestReviewMergeOutputFile updated to expect JSON summary on stdout.

tasks.title: Implement resolve: read-modify-write with flock
tasks.acceptance: review_resolve.go: reads NDJSON findings file, parses into array (0-indexed), adds resolved object {status, evidence, resolved_at} on target finding, acquires flock before write, writes back in-place. --force allows re-resolving already-resolved. --resolve-all applies to all unresolved (skips already-resolved unless --force). Evidence defaults to empty string when omitted. Duplicate status accepted. Exit codes: 3 file not found, 2 invalid index, 2 invalid status, 1 already resolved without --force. Tests: resolve by index, already-resolved error, force re-resolve, resolve-all, invalid index/status, preserves fields, omitted evidence, duplicate status.
tasks.closed_reason: review_resolve.go: runReviewResolve reads NDJSON, parses into map[string]any array, adds resolved object with status/evidence/resolved_at at target index, acquires flock before read-modify-write, writes in-place. runReviewResolveAll applies to all unresolved, skips already-resolved unless --force. Valid statuses: fixed/wontfix/duplicate. Exit 3 file not found, exit 2 invalid index/status, exit 1 already resolved without --force. Success output: stderr confirmation plus JSON to stdout. 8 tests covering add resolved, already-resolved error, force, resolve-all, invalid cases, preserves fields, omitted evidence.

tasks.title: Implement resolve success output
tasks.acceptance: On success: stderr shows 'resolved finding {N} as {status}'. With --json: stdout shows {index, status, evidence, file}. For --resolve-all: stderr shows 'resolved {N} findings as {status} ({M} already resolved, skipped)'. JSON includes {resolved_count, skipped_count, status, file}. resolve-all on fully-resolved file without --force: exit 0, 0 resolved, M skipped. Tests: success output, resolve-all output, fully-resolved no-op.
tasks.closed_reason: Resolve success output (stderr confirmation, JSON when piped) was implemented as part of resolve-core task in review_resolve.go. runReviewResolve prints 'resolved finding N as status' to stderr and JSON object to stdout. runReviewResolveAll prints 'resolved N findings as status (M already resolved, skipped)' to stderr and JSON summary to stdout.

tasks.title: Refactor buildFindingsSummary for compact mode
tasks.acceptance: buildFindingsSummary reads resolved field on findings. Fixed findings excluded from detailed listing but counted in resolved one-liner. Wontfix findings shown in detail with [WONTFIX] tag. Duplicate findings excluded entirely. Unresolved findings shown as before. Backward compatible: no resolved field = all unresolved. Section 1: detailed unresolved+wontfix. Section 2: 'N findings RESOLVED' one-liner. Tests: 10 unresolved + 50 fixed, all fixed, no resolved field (backward compat), wontfix tag, duplicate excluded.
tasks.closed_reason: buildFindingsSummary refactored: classifies findings by resolved status (fixed/duplicate excluded from detail, wontfix shown with [WONTFIX] tag, unresolved shown as before). Section 1: detailed unresolved+wontfix listing. Section 2: resolved count one-liner. Backward compatible: no resolved field = all unresolved. resolvedStatus struct added to reviewFinding. Tests updated: 'UNRESOLVED findings from previous rounds' replaces old header text.

tasks.title: Implement report cross-round analysis
tasks.acceptance: review_report.go: reads per-round NDJSON files (ordered by args or alphabetical from directory). Uses (category, location, finding_prefix) identity key. Computes per-round: in_file, new (not in UNION of all previous), resolved (in immediately preceding but not current), unresolved (cumulative), delta_percent. Convergence: true when last 2+ rounds have 0 findings. Severity breakdown with fixed/wontfix/duplicate/remaining. Reappearing finding counted as new. Tests: 3-file convergence, directory scan, converged/not converged, severity breakdown, delta calculation, reappearing finding.
tasks.closed_reason: review_report.go: runReviewReport reads per-round NDJSON files or directory (alphabetical), uses findingIdentityKey for cross-round tracking, computes per-round in_file/new/resolved/unresolved/delta_percent. Convergence: true when last 2+ rounds have 0 findings. Severity breakdown with fixed/wontfix/duplicate/remaining. TTY table output with convergence status. JSON output with rounds array and delta_percent (null for R1). 8 tests in review_report_test.go covering convergence, directory, severity breakdown, delta, empty dir.

tasks.title: Implement report TTY/JSON output formats
tasks.acceptance: TTY output: convergence table with In File, New, Resolved, Unresolved, Δ columns. Status line (CONVERGED/NOT CONVERGED). By severity and by category breakdowns. JSON output: rounds array with delta_percent field (null for R1), convergence object, by_severity with duplicate counts, by_category. Validation: 0 files exits 2, empty directory exits 2. Tests: TTY format, JSON structure, delta_percent null for R1, empty dir error.
tasks.closed_reason: TTY and JSON output formats both implemented in review_report.go as part of report-core. printReportTTY generates convergence table with columns, status line, severity and category breakdowns. JSON mode uses output.JSON with rounds array, convergence object, by_severity with duplicate counts.

tasks.title: Implement --spec-ref: omit inline spec
tasks.acceptance: When --spec-ref active: prompts contain 'Spec file: {path} ({N} lines, {M} sections)' and 'Read the spec file before reviewing' instead of inline spec content. Structured elements summary still inline. reviewResult gets spec_ref: true and spec_path with absolute path. review_loop.instruction includes 'Read the spec at {path}'. When combined with --diff-from, spec-ref is redundant (diff-from controls content). Tests: spec_ref and spec_path in output, prompt has no full spec, structured elements present, instruction text, diff-from takes priority.
tasks.closed_reason: When --spec-ref active: buildSpecRefContent generates 'Spec file: {absPath} ({N} lines, {M} sections)' with heading list. reviewResult gets spec_ref: true and spec_path with absolute path. review_loop.instruction includes 'Read the spec at {path}'. When combined with --diff-from, spec-ref is redundant (diff-from controls content via switch statement). New fields added to reviewResult struct. Validation: --diff-from requires --round >= 2, baseline must exist.

tasks.title: Create section-level spec diff engine
tasks.acceptance: engine/diff.go: DiffSections(baseLines, currentLines []string) returns ChangedSections (full text, MODIFIED/ADDED tag), RemovedSections (heading only), UnchangedSections (heading + element counts). Uses ParseHeadings to build section trees. Section = heading + content until next equal/higher heading. Heading-like lines inside fenced code blocks NOT treated as boundaries. Matches by heading text (exact after trim). Content comparison: line-by-line after trim, blank line count differences ignored. Renamed headings = deletion + addition (no rename detection). Tests: identical specs, added section, removed section, modified section, code block heading immunity, renamed heading.
tasks.closed_reason: DiffSections(baseLines, currentLines) in engine/diff.go returns DiffResult with Changed (MODIFIED/ADDED with full content), Removed (heading only), Unchanged (heading only) slices. parseSections splits lines by headings, skips heading-like lines inside fenced code blocks. sectionsEqual compares after normalizeContent (trim whitespace per line, collapse consecutive blanks). Matching by exact heading text after trim. Renamed headings treated as deletion+addition. Nil slices defaulted to empty. 10 tests in diff_test.go: identical, added, removed, modified, code block immunity, renamed heading, whitespace ignored, blank line diff ignored, empty input, multiple simultaneous changes.

tasks.title: Integrate diff engine into review prompts
tasks.acceptance: --diff-from flag accepted on review command. Requires --round >= 2 (exit 2 otherwise). Baseline file must exist (exit 3). Prompts contain 'Changed Sections' with full text (MODIFIED/ADDED), 'Removed Sections' listing, 'Unchanged Sections' with heading-only + element counts. Structured elements from changed sections get full check-each-row treatment, unchanged get heading-only. No differences detected: output includes hint. Tests: 1 changed section, added section, removed section, no changes hint, round 1 error, baseline not found, structured element treatment.
tasks.closed_reason: buildDiffSpecContent generates prompts with Changed Sections (full text, MODIFIED/ADDED), Removed Sections (heading only), Unchanged Sections (heading only) using engine.DiffSections. --diff-from flag validated: requires --round >= 2 (exit 2), baseline must exist (exit 3). No differences detected: output.Info hint. Spec content mode selection via switch statement: diffFrom, specRef, or default readSpecContent. Content capped at specContentCap.

tasks.title: Implement --verify lightweight verification
tasks.acceptance: review_verify.go: generates SINGLE prompt with role verifier, category verification. Classifies findings into fixed/wontfix/unresolved. Prompt adapts to counts (shows M addressed, W wontfix, U unresolved). Empty categories omitted from prompt. Includes resolution evidence for fixed findings. Regression category guidance in prompt. Supports --affected-files (same budget rules) and --diff-from. review_loop.mode = 'verification'. Instruction: 'If verifier finds 0 issues, review is complete'. Validation: requires --findings (exit 2), mutually exclusive with --perspective and --round (exit 2). NDJSON output format. Tests: all-fixed, mixed findings, no findings error, perspective error, round error, mode field, affected-files, diff-from.
tasks.closed_reason: review_verify.go: runReviewVerify generates SINGLE prompt with role verifier, category verification. Classifies findings into fixed/wontfix/unresolved via resolvedStatus. Prompt adapts to counts (shows M addressed, W wontfix, U unresolved). Empty categories omitted from prompt. Fixed findings include resolution evidence. Regression category guidance in prompt. Supports --affected-files (budget-aware via ReadAffectedFilesBudgetAware) and --diff-from (uses buildDiffSpecContent) and --spec-ref (uses buildSpecRefContent). review_loop.mode = verification. Instruction says 'If verifier finds 0 issues, review is complete'. Validation: requires --findings exit 2. NDJSON output guidance. 7 tests: all-fixed, mixed, no-findings error, mode field, affected-files, empty categories, regression guidance.

tasks.title: Update SKILL.md, README.md, CLAUDE.md
tasks.acceptance: SKILL.md documents --merge, --resolve, --resolve-all, --verify, --report, --spec-ref, --diff-from, --force, -o flags. Includes merge/resolve workflow example. README.md adds new flags and multi-round review workflow section. CLAUDE.md command reference updated with new flags.
tasks.closed_reason: SKILL.md: added multi-round review lifecycle section with merge/resolve/verify/report/diff-from/force commands, 6 new rows in Key Commands table. README.md: added 7 new review flags to command reference, new Multi-Round Review Workflow section with 6-step example and 9-row flag table. CLAUDE.md: added 7 new tp review lines to Spec and Validation section covering all new modes and flags.
